title: Identifier Management ‚Äî Non-Human Service Account Sprawl

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 IA-4 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 IA-4 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.14.28: Identifier Management ‚Äî Non-Human Service Account Sprawl.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.28' running on WinEventLog:Security 4741/4742, aws:cloudtrail CreateUser/CreateRole, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876)
spl:
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4741,4742) earliest=-30d
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by Account_Name, EventCode
| lookup svc_account_registry.csv Account_Name OUTPUT owner_team, approved
| where approved!="true" OR isnull(approved)
description: Tracks creation of service principals and machine accounts for IA-4 identifier management discipline.
value: Tracks creation of service principals and machine accounts for IA-4 identifier management discipline.
implementation: (1) Maintain authoritative registry CSV or KVStore; (2) integrate IAM ticketing for approvals; (3) monthly cleanup report; (4) align naming standards with CM-8 tags.
visualization: Table (unregistered service accounts), Time chart (creations), Bar chart (by OU)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We track creation of service principals and machine accounts for IA-4 identifier management discipline ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` 4741/4742, `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateUser`/`CreateRole`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Maintain authoritative registry CSV or KVStore; (2) integrate IAM ticketing for approvals; (3) monthly cleanup report; (4) align naming standards with CM-8 tags.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4741,4742) earliest=-30d
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by Account_Name, EventCode
| lookup svc_account_registry.csv Account_Name OUTPUT owner_team, approved
| where approved!="true" OR isnull(approved)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Identifier Management ‚Äî Non-Human Service Account Sprawl (IA-4)** ‚Äî Tracks creation of service principals and machine accounts for IA-4 identifier management discipline.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4741/4742, `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateUser`/`CreateRole`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sec; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=sec, sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by Account_Name, EventCode** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where approved!="true" OR isnull(approved)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Identifier Management ‚Äî Non-Human Service Account Sprawl (IA-4)** ‚Äî Tracks creation of service principals and machine accounts for IA-4 identifier management discipline.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` 4741/4742, `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateUser`/`CreateRole`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unregistered service accounts), Time chart (creations), Bar chart (by OU)
